up.method: dissolved H2 measured by GC-TCD/FID in source medium vs chemostat outflow; H2 identified as limiting factor
dissolved CO2(aq) by GC; carbon fixation

growth_records.cite: Francesco Smedile et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (mu measured from ln of cell density slope vs time at chemostat steady state); doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: at 20 MPa, the growth rate of strain PV-1 was 1.1 h-1 at 2 ml min-1 ... strain PV-1 grew more efficiently at 20 MPa (doubling time of ~16 min at 20 MPa and 55C)
growth_records.notes: HPC steady-state operating point (20 MPa, flow 2 ml/min): mu=1.1 h-1 reported directly. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/1.1 = 0.63 h (~38 min) at this operating dilution rate. (The ~16 min doubling quoted in text is at the MAXIMUM growth rate 2.61 h-1, captured as a separate record.) H2 established as growth-limiting substrate by comparing dissolved H2 in source medium vs outflow; no per-biomass uptake/secretion flux (mmol/gDW/h) reported. Source-medium electron donor/acceptor concentrations (H2 1.3, HCO3- ~7, NO3- ~5 mM) are from the primary shipboard enrichment at 25 MPa. Modified SME medium composition cited from Vetriani 2004 / Grosche 2015, not stated in this paper.

up.method: H2 identified as limiting substrate (source vs outflow GC)

growth_records.cite: Francesco Smedile et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (maximum dilution rate sustaining growth at 20 MPa; doubling ~16 min reported)
growth_records.snippet: The maximum dilution rates at which PV-1 sustained growth at 0.5 and 20 MPa were 1.60 and 2.61 h-1, respectively ... doubling time of ~16 min at 20 MPa and 55C ... shortest generation time of all known piezophilic bacteria
growth_records.notes: mu_max at 20 MPa = 2.61 h-1 (max dilution before washout), reported directly. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/2.61 = 0.266 h = ~16 min, matching the text-reported ~16 min. Experiment ran over 1633 h at pressures 0.4-20 MPa and dilution rates 0.06-2.61 h-1 (Table S2, not available). No numeric uptake/secretion flux reported.

up.method: H2 identified as limiting factor; LPC considered nutrient-depleted

growth_records.cite: Francesco Smedile et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (maximum dilution rate sustaining growth at 0.5 MPa)
growth_records.snippet: The maximum dilution rates at which PV-1 sustained growth at 0.5 and 20 MPa were 1.60 and 2.61 h-1, respectively ... its growth rate at 0.5 MPa dictated a lower flow rate of 0.2 ml min-1 to avoid cell washout
growth_records.notes: mu_max at 0.5 MPa = 1.60 h-1 (max dilution before washout), reported directly. Doubling time = ln2/1.60 = 0.693/1.60 = 0.433 h (~26 min). LPC proteomics operating point used flow 0.2 ml/min (much lower mu, not numerically stated; H2-limited). Strain grew more efficiently at 20 MPa than 0.5 MPa (piezophile).

growth_records.medium: modified SME medium supplemented with nitrate, H2/CO2 (80:20) headspace
growth_records.cite: Francesco Smedile et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively (batch growth used for proteomics; no numeric mu reported for LPB)
growth_records.snippet: We also investigated protein expression of strain PV-1 grown in batch culture at 0.2 MPa (LPB condition) ... the LPB condition replicated the procedure used to isolate strain PV-1.
growth_records.notes: LPB = low-pressure (0.2 MPa) batch condition; distinct experimental condition but no numeric growth/uptake/secretion rate reported (used for comparative proteomics vs LPC). Isolation batch cultures done at 55C, 0.2 MPa under H2/CO2 (80:20) headspace with modified SME + 10% w/v nitrate.
